product.name: Gloss
product.tagline: The word from your book — made clear in a breath
product.dek: Gloss sits beside a real book. Type a few letters (or say the word), get one short meaning with a picture, and get back to reading. No account, no ads, no tracking — everything stays on this device.
product.note: Below is what a child or parent actually meets, step by step — look up a word, keep a list of words you've met, and quiz yourself when you're ready.

videoNote: Internal: GitHub Release assets only. See docs/DEMO-VIDEOS.md.

parts.title: From a hard word to a clear meaning
parts.dek: Phone on the table, book in hand. Find the word, skip anything you don't need, read the meaning, move on.

features.title: What word?
features.summary: Home opens on a big “What word?” field. Type two or three letters and suggestions appear right away. The top bar shows how many words you've met, who's reading, and About.

features.title: Helpful suggestions as you type
features.summary: Each suggestion shows a little color cue for the kind of word it is (a naming word, a doing word, a describing word…), the word itself, and a plain label a child can understand.

features.title: A line from the book — optional
features.summary: After you pick a word, Gloss can take a line from the page so the meaning matches the story. Skip it anytime; many words are clear without it.

features.title: One short meaning, with a picture
features.summary: The word is large and easy to see, with a picture (or simple motion) and one kid-plain sentence. Tap Speak to hear it. The word is quietly saved to this reader's list.

features.title: Another word — and back to the book
features.summary: One tap returns to a clean home. Every word you look up stays on this device for later review or a quiz.

parts.title: Several readers on one device — no accounts
parts.dek: Siblings (or a parent and child) can each keep their own word list. No email, no cloud, no sign-in.

features.title: Who's reading?
features.summary: Tap the colored avatar to switch readers or add another (You, Reader 2, …). Lists stay separate on this phone or tablet.

features.title: Words you've met
features.summary: The bookmark shows how many words this reader has looked up. Open the list to see a meaning again. Empty state: “No words yet — look one up and it lands here for later.”

features.title: Quiz me
features.summary: Once there are at least three words, Gloss shows a picture and asks “Which word?” with three choices. A short quiz, a score, play again — all offline from words you've already met.

parts.title: Say the word, miss a spelling, hand the phone over
parts.dek: Typing always works. Speech is there when it helps. Parents use the phone's own pin tools — not a separate app password.

features.title: Say it out loud
features.summary: Tap the mic under the word field if speaking is easier. Listening only starts when you ask — typing stays the main way in.

features.title: Not in our word pack yet
features.summary: If a spelling isn't known yet, Gloss says so gently: “Hmm, not in our word pack yet.” Try again with another guess — no dead ends or ads.

features.title: About, privacy, hand to child
features.summary: The small “i” opens About: the offline promise, Privacy and Terms, Support, and simple steps to pin the app for a child (Guided Access on iPhone, screen pinning on Android).

parts.title: Warm, calm, and clear on every screen
parts.dek: Soft paper colors, a gentle green, and meanings that show the right kind of picture for the word — a thing, an action, a before-and-after, and more.

features.title: Soft paper and quiet color
features.summary: A warm paper background, soft green for the main actions, and a golden Speak button. Easy to look at next to a real book at the table.

features.title: Pictures that match the kind of word
features.summary: Naming words get a picture. Doing words can show motion. Describing words can show before and after. The goal is always: see it, get it, keep reading.

features.title: Works without the internet
features.summary: Words and meanings live on the device. Lookups don't need Wi‑Fi or a data plan — ideal on the sofa, in the car, or at a cabin.

journeys.name: Look up a word by typing
steps: Skip the line from the book if you don't need it
Read the meaning — or tap Speak
Tap Another word and go back to the book

journeys.name: Use a line from the book
steps: Type a word that can mean more than one thing
Add a short line from the page
See how the meaning fits the story

journeys.name: When a spelling isn't known yet
steps: Type a guess that isn't in the pack

journeys.name: Hand the phone to a child
steps: Follow the simple pin steps for your phone

journeys.name: Practice words you've met
steps: Look up a few words (at least three)
Pick the word that matches the picture
Switch readers anytime with the avatar
